profile.name: Jame
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to take the lead on meeting flow and big-picture priorities, but leans on Mary for account specifics.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate — comfortable discussing goals, taxes, and tradeoffs, but prefers the advisor to translate products and account mechanics into plain language.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but protection-first — will accept some market movement if the plan clearly supports capital preservation and predictable spending; gets uneasy when outcomes feel open-ended.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-driven; uses phrases like “I think” and “roughly,” sometimes mixes up gross vs. net or balance vs. payment, and appreciates quick check-backs and summaries.
emotional_tendencies: worries about healthcare and long-term care costs
relief when there is a clear plan and checklist
profile.decision_making_style: Starts with the goal (security), then asks for 2–3 options; prefers to sleep on decisions and come back with follow-up questions.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open — willing to engage, but wants transparency on fees, risks, and what happens in a down market before fully committing.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers not to move accounts unless the benefit is obvious)
hidden_concerns: outliving assets despite relatively low ongoing income sources
being a burden to Mary if health declines (he describes his health as fair)

profile.name: Mar
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; the “numbers person” who tracks statements and corrects details, but lets James set priorities and speak first.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high — reads statements, notices fees and interest rates, and can follow allocation/tax discussions; may not enjoy jargon but can handle it when explained.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a conservative tilt — prefers steadier outcomes and is quick to question anything that could jeopardize capital preservation; will push back on complexity (especially alternatives) unless clearly justified.
profile.communication_style: Precise and detail-oriented; will cite exact figures when she has them, asks clarifying questions, and occasionally interrupts to correct small inaccuracies; less hedging than James but still uses “let me check” when unsure.
emotional_tendencies: becomes uneasy when details don’t reconcile
feels reassured by documentation and clear next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Verifies facts first, then evaluates pros/cons; prefers written follow-ups and a structured plan before agreeing to changes.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Measured — trust increases quickly when the advisor demonstrates accuracy, consistency, and respect for their preference for capital preservation.
hidden_concerns: maintaining independence and avoiding financial surprises as they age (she describes her health as good, but plans ahead)
tax drag given their 32% tax bracket band
